source_display: Narrowness Paradox (Every Time Too Narrow = Still Too Broad)
evidence: One of my big learnings in retrospect is every time I thought we were being too narrow, we were still being too broad.

source_display: PMF Obsession Test (When you have it you don't think about it)
evidence: Product market fit is when you have it, you don't think about it. And when you don't have it, you fucking thinking about it all the time.

source_display: Linchpin Deal Strategy (Design Partner > Death by 1000 Pilots)
evidence: Seek 'linchpin deals' or design partners—organizations willing to pay something for a pilot with limited custom feature requests rather than death by a thousand cuts through endless pilots.
Strategic advisory conversation between Vrijen and Chris (special education teacher turned edtech founder building ADHD/executive function coaching product).

source_display: Hiring Systems as Dial-Up (When Broadband Needed)
evidence: Outdated hiring systems described as 'dial-up when broadband is needed' that haven't evolved to process richer signals about candidates.

source_display: Mental Model Gap (Not Tech Problem, Thinking Problem)
evidence: You do not have a technology problem. You have a mental model problem... It's not that you lack the tools or even the knowledge, it's that people are collecting individual techniques without actually understanding how they fit.

source_display: LinkedIn Hidden Data (Shows 10% of What They Have)
evidence: LinkedIn has everything that you have ever done, but they only show you 10% of it.

source_display: Friends of Next Play AI Workshop
evidence: Public workshop on 'Fundamentals of AI Productivity' led by Vrijen Attawar, Logan Currie, and Ben Guo.
